etymology.description: 辰(辰) (chén, 'agricultural tool') serves as phonetic -> combines with 虫(虫) (chóng, 'insect') as semantic -> 蜃(shèn, 'large clam')
etymology.interpretation: The character 蜃(shèn) combines 辰(辰) (chén, 'agricultural tool'), which acts as the phonetic component, with 虫(虫) (chóng, 'insect'), serving as the semantic component indicating a creature. The overall meaning relates to a large clam, reflecting the initially specific association with marine life rather than insects.

examples.chinese: 海边发现了一只蜃(海邊發現了一隻蜃)，它的壳闪闪发光(它的殼閃閃發光)。
examples.pinyin: Hǎi biān fāxiàn le yì zhī shèn, tā de ké shǎnshǎn fāguāng.
examples.english: By the sea, a giant clam was discovered, and its shell shimmered.

examples.chinese: 老渔夫捕捉到一只蜃(老漁夫捕捉到一隻蜃)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎo yúfū bǔzhuō dào yì zhī shèn.
examples.english: An old fisherman caught a giant clam.

examples.chinese: 传说中蜃的出现预示着海洋风暴的来临(傳說中蜃的出現預示著海洋風暴的來臨)。
examples.pinyin: Chuánshuō zhōng shèn de chūxiàn yùshì zhe hǎiyáng fēngbào de láilín.
examples.english: According to legend, the appearance of a giant clam foretells the coming of an ocean storm.
